title: Ranunculus macilentus
intro: Ranunculus macilentus, also known as the slender buttercup, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Ranunculaceae family, characterized by its delicate white or pale yellow flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in traditional medicine for treating various ailments such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Ranunculus macilentus include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, including ranunculin and protoanemonin, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Ranunculus macilentus include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Ranunculus macilentus is classified in the family Ranunculaceae and in the order Ranunculales. It belongs to the kingdom Plantae and the clade Angiosperms. Furthermore, it is also classified as a member of the clade Eudicots and the clade Monocots. The classification is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Monocots, order Ranunculales, family Ranunculaceae, and genus Ranunculus. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships with other species.

preparations: Ranunculus macilentus is a species of flowering plant that has been used in traditional herbal medicine, although its use should be approached with caution due to the presence of toxic compounds. side_effects: Ranunculus macilentus, commonly known as the thread-leaved buttercup, contains toxic compounds known as ranunculin and other alkaloids. Ingestion of the plant can cause severe gastrointestinal symptoms, such as nausea and vomiting, and can also lead to more serious issues like kidney and liver damage. Contact with the plant's sap can cause skin irritation and blistering. In severe cases, ingesting Ranunculus macilentus can even lead to cardiac arrest and death. It's essential to avoid handling or consuming the plant to prevent these adverse effects.
